preamble: The shift from an aspirated voiced alveolar stop to a voiced alveolar affricate represents a process of assibilation, common in various historical lenition paths or Indo-Aryan developments.

examples.note: Historical stages in Indo-Aryan or Germanic lenition where aspirated stops undergo affrication.

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
